source_paper_title: Tightness Without Counterexamples: A New Approach and New Results for Prophet Inequalities
context:
Page 6, Section 1.2 (New Results), item 2

It remains a challenging problem to analytically verify for all \(k > 1\) our numerical observation that \(\lim_{n\to\infty} \gamma_{k,n} = \inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{k,n}\), which would resolve the open question of \(k\)-unit IID prophet inequality.
title: Prove that the worst-case IID \\(k\\)-unit prophet-inequality ratio occurs as \\(n\\to\\infty\\) for all \\(k>1\\)
background_latex:
Consider the IID multi-unit prophet inequality problem with \(k\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) selection slots and \(n\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) sequentially arriving agents. Each agent \(i\in\{1,\dots,n\}\) has a nonnegative random value \(R_i\), and in the IID setting \(R_1,\dots,R_n\) are independent and identically distributed.

Let \(\mathrm{DP}(I)\) denote the expected value of an optimal online policy (equivalently, the optimal dynamic program) for instance \(I\), and let \(\mathrm{Proph}(I)\) denote the expected value obtained by a prophet who observes all realizations in advance and selects up to \(k\) agents with maximum total value.

Let \(\mathcal{I}^{\mathrm{IID}}_{k,n}\) be the class of all such IID instances with parameters \((k,n)\). Define the tight competitive ratio
\[\gamma_{k,n} := \inf_{I\in \mathcal{I}^{\mathrm{IID}}_{k,n}} \frac{\mathrm{DP}(I)}{\mathrm{Proph}(I)}.\]
Define also
\[\underline{\gamma}_k := \inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{k,n}, \qquad \overline{\gamma}_k := \lim_{n\to\infty} \gamma_{k,n}\]
whenever the limit exists (e.g., as a \(\liminf\) if needed).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove (or disprove) that for every integer \(k>1\), the worst-case IID \(k\)-unit prophet-inequality ratio is attained in the limit of infinitely many agents, i.e.
\[\overline{\gamma}_k = \underline{\gamma}_k,\]
equivalently,
\[\lim_{n\to\infty} \gamma_{k,n} = \inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{k,n}.\]
latex:
Consider the IID multi-unit prophet inequality problem with \(k\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) selection slots and \(n\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}\) sequentially arriving agents. Each agent \(i\in\{1,\dots,n\}\) has a nonnegative random value \(R_i\), and in the IID setting \(R_1,\dots,R_n\) are independent and identically distributed.

Let \(\mathrm{DP}(I)\) denote the expected value of an optimal online policy (equivalently, the optimal dynamic program) for instance \(I\), and let \(\mathrm{Proph}(I)\) denote the expected value obtained by a prophet who observes all realizations in advance and selects up to \(k\) agents with maximum total value.

Let \(\mathcal{I}^{\mathrm{IID}}_{k,n}\) be the class of all such IID instances with parameters \((k,n)\). Define the tight competitive ratio
\[\gamma_{k,n} := \inf_{I\in \mathcal{I}^{\mathrm{IID}}_{k,n}} \frac{\mathrm{DP}(I)}{\mathrm{Proph}(I)}.\]
Define also
\[\underline{\gamma}_k := \inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{k,n}, \qquad \overline{\gamma}_k := \lim_{n\to\infty} \gamma_{k,n}\]
whenever the limit exists (e.g., as a \(\liminf\) if needed).

Prove (or disprove) that for every integer \(k>1\), the worst-case IID \(k\)-unit prophet-inequality ratio is attained in the limit of infinitely many agents, i.e.
\[\overline{\gamma}_k = \underline{\gamma}_k,\]
equivalently,
\[\lim_{n\to\infty} \gamma_{k,n} = \inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{k,n}.\]
open_problem_quote: It remains a challenging problem to analytically verify for all \(k > 1\) our numerical observation that \(\lim_{n\to\infty} \gamma_{k,n} = \inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{k,n}\), which would resolve the open question of \(k\)-unit IID prophet inequality.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
